hypothesis: EMA-based loss normalization solves the 2,600x scale imbalance between L1 reward and SupCon hand losses
key_finding: EMA normalization overcorrected: reward loss achieved Spearman rho=0.672 (best ever) but hand accuracy collapsed to 38.3% (near chance). Loss-magnitude normalization does not equalize gradient contributions because reward loss has a more favorable gradient landscape after convergence.
next_steps: Try gradient norm balancing (GradNorm) instead of loss magnitude
Try high lambda_hand (e.g., 500-1000x) to counteract reward gradient dominance
Try separate embedding subspaces for reward and hand axes
